channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russian missiles hit Ukrainian city of Sumy during Palm Sunday celebrations, killing more than 30
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — Russian missiles struck the heart of the Ukrainian city of Sumy as people gathered to celebrate Palm Sunday, killing at least 34 people, officials said, in the second large-scale attack to claim civilian lives in just over a week. The two ballistic missiles hit around 10:15 a.m.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Home to centuries of heritage, Sudan's biggest museum is looted and wrecked by a 2-year war
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Inside Sudan's biggest museum, the exhibition halls once filled with statues and relics from centuries of ancient civilizations are trashed, littered with debris. The display cases stand empty and shattered. A mummy lies exposed in an open storage box.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China's Xi says there are no winners in a tariff war as he visits Southeast Asia
item.description: China’s leader Xi Jinping says no one wins in a trade war as he kicked off a diplomatic tour of Southeast Asia with a trip to Vietnam.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Aniruddha Ghosal, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 6 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen’s rebel-held capital killed at least six people and wounded 26 overnight.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mario Vargas Llosa, Peruvian author and Nobel literature laureate, dies at 89
item.description: Peruvian author Mario Vargas Llosa, Nobel literature laureate and a giant of Latin American letters for many decades, has died, his son said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Franklin Briceño And Ciarán Giles, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump admin secures release of American missionary held in Tunisia for 13 months: report
item.description: American missionary Robert Vieira, detained in Tunisia for more than a year, is set to return home soon after officials secured his release on Sunday, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio says another 10 people have been sent to El Salvador
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Sunday that another 10 people have been sent to El Salvador amid the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. “Last night, another 10 criminals from the MS-13 and Tren de Aragua Foreign Terrorist Organizations arrived in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Kellogg condemns Russia's attack on Palm Sunday gathering in Ukraine
item.description: Keith Kellogg, President Trump’s special envoy to Ukraine, condemned Russia’s attack on a Palm Sunday gathering in Ukraine. “Today's Palm Sunday attack by Russian forces on civilian targets in Sumy crosses any line of decency,” Kellogg said in a post on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: At least 50 people killed in east Congo as government and Rwanda-backed rebels trade blame
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 50 people were killed in weekend attacks in Congo’s conflict-battered east, authorities said Saturday. The government traded blame with Rwanda-backed rebels over who was responsible for the violence that quickly escalated the conflict in the region.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and JEAN-YVES KAMALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's new leader makes his first visit to the United Arab Emirates
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa made his first visit Sunday to the United Arab Emirates, whose leaders have been circumspect about the new leadership in Damascus in the four months since the fall of former President Bashar Assad in a lightning rebel offensive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gabon’s coup leader Oligui Nguema elected president with 90% of votes cast, provisional results show
item.description: LIBREVILLE, Gabon (AP) — Gen. Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema, Gabon’s interim president who staged a 2023 coup that ended a decadeslong political dynasty, has been elected president, according to provisional results announced by the country’s interior ministry.
item.dc:creator: YVES LAURENT GOMA and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump admin makes new move to bring South African refugees to US as president blasts nation's rulers again
item.description: As the U.S. begins refugee processing for South African Afrikaners, a group is pushing Washington to support its bid for an autonomous Afrikaner homeland within South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daniel Noboa is reelected Ecuador's president by voters weary of crime
item.description: Ecuadorian voters have reelected President Daniel Noboa, a conservative young millionaire with a divisive no-holds-barred crimefighting record.
item.dc:creator: Gonzalo Solano And Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How a penguin in a cardboard box caused a helicopter crash in South Africa
item.description: A penguin inside a carboard box led to a helicopter crash in South Africa, the country's Civil Aviation Authority said in a report this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Dominican club collapse death toll rises to 226 after woman pulled from rubble dies in hospital
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A woman hospitalized after being rescued from the rubble of a roof collapse at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic died on Saturday, raising the death toll to 226, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attacks on famine-hit camps in Sudan's Darfur leave at least 100 people dead, UN official says
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan's notorious paramilitary group launched a two-day attack on famine-hit camps for displaced people that left more than 100 dead, including 20 children and nine aid workers, in the Darfur region, a U.N. official said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran, US complete first round of 'constructive' nuclear talks in Oman
item.description: Envoys from Iran and the U.S. completed the first round of “positive” and “constructive” talks regarding Tehran’s developing nuclear program in Oman and agreed to continue discussions next weekend, according to the Trump administration. Middle East envoy Steve Wiktoff, alongside U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Mexico to send water to Texas amid Trump tariff threats
item.description: Mexico will facilitate an “immediate” delivery of water to farmers in Texas to help shore up resources amid President Trump’s tariff threats, Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: DHS ends protections from deportation for Afghanistan, Cameroon
item.description: Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem has stripped protections from deportation for Afghans and Cameroonians in the U.S., calling into question the ability of some Afghan evacuees to remain in America.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: European countries vow billions in military support for Ukraine as US envoy meets Putin
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European countries vowed Friday to sends billions of dollars in further funding to help Ukraine keep fighting Russia’s invasion, as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Harry makes visit to Ukraine, meets with wounded veterans
item.description: Prince Harry is touching down in Ukraine, getting a first-hand look at a rehabilitation center in the war-torn country. The Duke of Sussex visited the Superhumans Center in Lviv on Thursday, a spokesperson confirmed to ITK.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The UN decries ongoing combat in Myanmar as earthquake relief faces big challenges
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Human rights experts for the United Nations are expressing urgent concern about ongoing military operations in Myanmar’s civil war, despite ceasefires called by major parties to facilitate relief efforts after the country’s devastating March 28 earthquake. At the same time, a new U.N.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan's president targets China influence, kicks out pro-Beijing agitators amid rising tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President William Lai promises an uncompressing stance against both domestic and foreign pro-CCP influence operations amid rising tensions between Taipei and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama and US lock in new security pact for Canal as China tensions simmer
item.description: Panama and the U.S. signed a new security pact on the canal, boosting defense ties as tensions with China over Latin American influence continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation
